rationale: The page contains detailed technical information about Tokamak reactors and Fast Breeder Reactors (FBR), including India's specific projects like ADITYA and the PFBR at Kalpakkam. This is high-value content for generating questions on nuclear energy and science policy.
cleanedText:
About Tokamak Reactor
* Purpose: Designed to harness the energy of fusion.
* Working: In it, fusion plasma is created and confined by strong magnetic fields.
* Plasma is a fundamental state of matter along with solids, liquids and gases.
* Other Major Tokamak Reactor: Joint European Torus (JET) in UK, Experimental Advanced Superconducting Tokamak (EAST) in China, Korea Superconducting Tokamak Advanced Research (KSTAR) in South Korea, JT-60SA (built and operated by Japan and Europe in Japan).
India’s activities in nuclear fusion and Tokamak Research
* ADITYA: First indigenously designed and built tokamak by Institute of plasma research.
* ADITYA-U Tokamak: A medium-size air-core tokamak.
* Steadystate Superconducting Tokamak-1 (SST-1): Medium size superconducting tokamak
7.1.2. FAST BREEDER REACTOR
Why in the News?
India’s first Fast-Breeder Nuclear Reactor (500 MWe) is set for commissioning by 2026.
About Fast Breeder Reactor
* Genesis: Government established Bharatiya Nabhikiya Vidyut Nigam Limited (BHAVINI) to construct and operate Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR).
* Functioning:
* Uses indigenously produced Uranium-Plutonium Mixed Oxide (MOX) fuel and utilize liquid sodium as a coolant.
* Uranium-238 blanket surrounding fuel core will undergo nuclear transmutation to produce more fuel, thus earning the name ‘Breeder’.
* Location: Kalpakkam, Tamil Nadu
Significance of FBR
* Technological Advancement: Once commissioned, India will only be the second country after Russia to have commercial operating FBR.
* Utilization of Thorium Reserve: The operationalization of PFBR will mark the start of stage II of India’s three-stage nuclear power program.
* By transmutation, Thorium will create fissile U-233 which will be used as fuel in the third stage.
* Safety: passive safety features ensuring a prompt and safe shut down of the plant in the event of an emergency.
